page: Amazon Web Services m6in.8xlarge is a general-purpose, network-optimized server instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture with an Intel Xeon 8375C processor running at 3.5 GHz. The instance provides 32 vCPUs across 16 physical cores with hyperthreading enabled, supported by a 54 MB L3 cache. It is equipped with 128.0 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz, yielding 4.0 GB of RAM per core. This instance does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, relying instead on network-attached storage.

Performance benchmarks place the m6in.8xlarge in the average tier for most compute and memory tasks. It achieves a Geekbench single-core score of 1654.0 and a multi-core score of 13933.0, alongside a Passmark CPU score of 36724.04. Memory bandwidth tests show 1657.93 GB/sec for cached small-block reads and 150.47 GB/sec for uncached large-block reads. In specialized workloads, the instance demonstrates strong performance in text generation for small language models with 135M parameters at 262.65 tokens/sec, though it drops to a weak performance level of 6.22 tokens/sec for prompt processing on large 70B models.

The instance is optimized for network-heavy operations, offering a baseline network bandwidth of 50 Gbps. From a cost-efficiency perspective, the m6in.8xlarge offers a balanced resource density of compute and memory, making it suitable for workloads that require high network throughput without the need for local NVMe storage. Appropriate workloads include static web serving, database operations, software compilation, and moderate-scale language model inference.
description: Amazon Web Services m6in.8xlarge is a general-purpose, network-optimized instance built on the Nitro hypervisor. It features an Intel Xeon 8375C processor with 32 vCPUs, 16 physical cores, and a 3.5 GHz clock speed, supported by 128.0 GB of DDR4 memory. The instance lacks local storage and dedicated GPUs, relying on network-attached storage and offering a high baseline network bandwidth of 50 Gbps. Benchmark results place its compute and memory capabilities in the average tier, with a Passmark CPU score of 36724.04 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 13933.0. It demonstrates strong performance in text generation for small language models, but exhibits weak performance when processing prompts for larger 70B models. This hardware profile is well-suited for network-intensive applications, static web serving, database operations, and general-purpose enterprise workloads.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m6in.8xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 32 vCPUs, 128 GB DDR4 RAM, and a 3.5 GHz Intel Xeon processor. It offers 50 Gbps baseline network bandwidth and lacks local storage.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m6in.8xlarge is a general-purpose instance with 32 vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and 50 Gbps network bandwidth, powered by an Intel Xeon CPU.
tagline: A network-optimized general-purpose instance featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors and high-bandwidth networking for balanced enterprise workloads.

Optimized for EBS and network-heavy enterprise applications

No local storage or dedicated GPU hardware included
